pricing_analysis.conclusion: TPL is an exceptional asset system. After marking its complete royalty acreage to actual royalty transactions and raising the surface-land cross-check, the quote is above the risked base value but below the high economic case. The market still requires strong royalty, produced-water, operated-water, and strategic-project execution, so the security remains a watch rather than a rejection of the asset quality.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: The component model may still understate repeated monetization of the same acreage, increasing water intensity, and data-center or power demand that converts faster than the milestone probabilities assume.
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: One post-December-2025-split common share has the same economic claim as every other common share. Q1 2026 diluted shares were approximately 69.0 million; TPL had net cash and an undrawn credit facility.

wisdom_applied: Horizon Kinetics: value the capital-free royalty structure and dormant assets, not their GAAP carrying amount.
Horizon Kinetics/PrairieSky: third parties fund drilling, so acreage content and cash flow can expand without owner capital.
Groundbreaker: the same non-depleting surface can host sequential tolls, but each toll is separated to prevent double counting.
Klarman: current cash flows, comparable asset NAV, and uncontracted catalysts remain distinct layers.
limitations: TPL does not disclose a tract-level split between producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant NRA; the residual reconciliation is portfolio-level.

Royalty transaction values vary dramatically with production, operator, location, and inventory depth; the $75k high mark remains below developed Viper/Sitio marks but above TPL's recent acquisition marks.
Data-center and desalination success values are not comparable NAV and remain probability weighted.

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: The current price may capitalize premium royalty and water growth while the undeveloped inventory and infrastructure options are neither independently measurable nor contractually realizable.
unresolved_items: No complete public royalty-versus-operated-water volume, margin, and capital bridge exists.
No tract/county inventory reconciles total NRA to producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant buckets.

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

gaps.question: How much royalty acreage is economically developable, at what royalty interest, and on what cadence?
gaps.evidence_required: Tract-level or county-level acreage, royalty-interest, operator activity, well inventory, and development-cadence reconciliation.
gaps.acceptance_test: Every acreage bucket has a unique ownership claim, activity class, probability, timing range, and no overlap with producing royalty cash flow.
gaps.valuation_effect: Re-estimates undeveloped royalty quantity, probability, and timing rather than applying one blanket per-acre value.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: FY2025 10-K confirms approximately 224,000 total NRA, comprising 1/128 NPRI under approximately 85,000 acres, 1/16 NPRI under approximately 371,000 acres, and approximately 33,000 additional 1/8-normalized NRA (TPL/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json). The filing still lacks a tract/county roll-forward allocating each claim to producing, visible undeveloped, or dormant acreage with activity, probability, and timing.

gaps.question: Which optional projects have enforceable milestones, and how much owner capital remains before cash realization?
gaps.evidence_required: Contracts, counterparties, permitting, power and water commitments, milestone dates, ownership share, and remaining owner-funded capital.
gaps.acceptance_test: Each option has a dated milestone tree, success probability, time-to-cash range, capital requirement, and zero value when the first falsifier occurs.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
routing_reasons: existing methods=land, option, replacement, royalty, unit_nav
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Producing oil and gas royalties
components.assumption_summary: Capital-free royalty cash flow. Base: 4% revenue growth in years 1-5, 2.5% in years 6-10, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 22x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: Do not add undeveloped royalty inventory that is already contributing to the $411.7M producing run-rate.

components.label: Existing surface, easement, and land-use operations
components.assumption_summary: Base: 6% growth in years 1-5 and 3.5% thereafter, 65% after-tax owner-cash conversion, 20x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: Named uncontracted corridors and data-center projects remain in separate option components.

components.label: Produced-water royalty stream
components.assumption_summary: Capital-free stream. Base: 12% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 5% thereafter, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 24x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: The base growth rate is far below the 2018-2025 historical volume CAGR and must be tested against basin oil volumes, water-oil ratios, AMI coverage, and realized royalty per barrel.

components.label: Cash and equity-method investment
components.cross_check: No debt is disclosed in the FY2025 balance-sheet bridge.

components.label: Operated water sales and services
components.assumption_summary: Base: 10% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 4% thereafter, 35% after-tax owner-cash margin after maintenance capital, 30% incremental after-tax ROIC, 18x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: The model charges growth investment explicitly; higher growth creates value only when the assumed incremental after-tax return on capital is earned.

components.label: Contracted data-center land-financing receivable
components.cross_check: Uncontracted data-center and power development remains in the separate project option.

components.label: Visible undeveloped royalty inventory
components.assumption_summary: 45% of the comparable-NAV residual after subtracting producing royalty value; the 70,000-NRA label remains provisional until a tract-level inventory roll-forward is disclosed.
components.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory reconcile to the total comparable value of all 224,000 NRA, so the same royalty acre cannot be capitalized twice.

components.label: Dormant and long-dated royalty inventory
components.assumption_summary: 55% of the comparable-NAV residual; duration and tract-quality uncertainty are expressed through the portfolio unit marks rather than an arbitrary zero value.
components.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory equal approximately $81/$130/$243 per share, corresponding to $25k/$40k/$75k per total NRA.

components.label: Residual uncontracted surface land
components.assumption_summary: 882,000 acres at $1,500/$3,000/$5,718 per acre less 20%/15%/10% portfolio realization friction.
components.cross_check: Per-acre marks represent only residual value after recurring operations and named project options; they are not a second capitalization of existing easement cash flow.

components.label: Future infrastructure corridors and easements
components.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted successful-project PV less remaining development cost.
components.cross_check: Each project graduates into existing surface/easement operations once contracted.

components.label: Data-center, power, and water co-location option
components.assumption_summary: Success value is probability-weighted for customer, power, interconnection, water, capital, permitting, and commissioning milestones.
components.cross_check: The separately modeled receivable and the balance-sheet value of the Bolt investment are excluded from this incremental option.

components.label: Desalination and water-technology platform
components.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted platform value less remaining commercialization cost; the asset state is demonstration, not research-only.
components.cross_check: Commercial value requires repeatable unit economics and a capital-light license, cost-plus, JV, or spin structure; technical success alone is insufficient.

components.label: Corporate, tax, and realization reserve
components.cross_check: Low case absorbs more of the gross option value as realization cost.

components.label: Water infrastructure replacement-cost cross-check
components.cross_check: The operated-water DCF already capitalizes returns on installed infrastructure.
